story_arc.title: Raw Song
story_arc.theme_ko: 상실을 품고 걷던 주인공이 반복되는 도시의 신호와 빛을 지나며 스스로 앞으로 나아갈 이유를 발견하는 이야기
story_arc.logline_ko: 'Raw Song'은 종이학을 든 외로운 청소년 주인공이 주요 공간인 비가 그친 조용한 도시 거리를 지나며 실루엣, 바람, 멀리 보이는 도시 불빛, 텅 빈 거리 같은 상징을 단서처럼 따라가고, 마지막에는 네온 마젠타와 사이버 핑크 빛 속에서 작은 결심을 되찾는 사이버 애니메이션 MV입니다.
story_arc.story_summary_ko: 이 뮤직비디오는 쓸쓸하고 서정적인 감정에서 출발합니다. 주인공은 같은 옷차림과 종이학을 지닌 채 비에 젖은 도시를 걷고, 각 장면은 도망치거나 멈춰 서는 대신 조금씩 앞으로 이동하는 행동으로 이어집니다. 벌스에서는 아직 말하지 못한 감정을 품고 걷고, 프리코러스에서는 신호등과 빛의 변화가 내면의 망설임을 흔듭니다. 코러스에서는 카메라와 걸음이 함께 앞으로 밀려 나가며 감정이 커지고, 마지막 아웃트로에서는 종이학과 악센트 빛이 남아 주인공이 과거를 지우지 않은 채 다음 아침으로 넘어간다는 결말을 만듭니다.

acts.name: 시작
acts.purpose_ko: 주인공, 소품, 세계의 감정을 소개하고 아직 멈춰 있는 상태를 보여줍니다.

acts.name: 전개
acts.purpose_ko: 가사와 리듬을 따라 주인공이 도시를 이동하며 감정의 단서를 마주합니다.

acts.name: 고조
acts.purpose_ko: 코러스와 반복 구간에서 같은 공간을 더 강한 빛과 움직임으로 통과하며 결심이 선명해집니다.

acts.name: 결말
acts.purpose_ko: 종이학과 악센트 빛을 통해 감정이 정리되고 다음 장면 이후의 여운을 남깁니다.

continuity_rules: 모든 씬은 같은 주인공, 같은 헤어스타일, 같은 의상, 같은 종이학 소품을 유지합니다.
각 씬의 이동 방향은 이전 씬의 감정에서 다음 씬의 감정으로 이어지는 흐름을 보여줍니다.
채널 브랜드 팔레트와 네온 마젠타와 사이버 핑크 중심의 사이버 컬러 규칙은 전체 영상에서 유지됩니다.
씬 전환은 비, 반사, 종이학, 신호등, 도시의 빛 같은 반복 심볼을 이용합니다.

visual_world.visual_identity: dark cyber noir anime with neon magenta and cyber pink dominance

color_palette.rule: limited-color cyber anime palette: neon magenta/cyber pink dominant, deep plum/dark violet shadows, graphite gray/near-black backgrounds, subtle icy cyan reflections only, and silver-white highlights

visual_world.lighting_language: dark cinematic cyber lighting, neon magenta glow, deep violet shadows, subtle icy cyan reflections, silver rim light
visual_world.transition_language: match cuts through neon reflections, paper crane motion, waveform-like light trails, and passing train lights

protagonist.identity: solitary anime teenager carrying a folded paper crane
protagonist.age_style: late-teen anime character, stylized and non-photorealistic
protagonist.hair: short layered black hair with a long side fringe, slightly wind-tossed
protagonist.outfit: dark school coat over a white shirt, narrow tie, simple shoulder bag
protagonist.silhouette: slim figure, coat hem and side fringe readable in silhouette
protagonist.accent_detail: neon magenta/cyber pink is dominant, with deep plum shadows, subtle icy cyan reflections, and silver-white rim highlights
consistency_rules: Keep the same hairstyle in every scene.
Keep the same outfit and shoulder bag in every scene.
Preserve the paper crane as the recurring symbol.
Use anime cinematic styling, never live-action realism.
First create a full character turnaround model sheet from character_reference_prompt.md.
The model sheet must show front, left side, right side, back, and three-quarter views.
Attach that same character model sheet when generating every scene image.
Attach the final scene image as the primary image-to-video reference when generating the video for that scene.
If the video tool supports multiple references, also attach the character model sheet as a secondary identity reference.
reference_workflow: Generate a clean character turnaround model sheet before scene production.
The model sheet should include front, back, left side, right side, three-quarter view, full body, and face close-up references.
Use the same model sheet as an input/reference for every scene image prompt.
After each scene image is approved, use that scene image as the primary image-to-video input with the matching video prompt.
When possible, include the model sheet as a secondary reference during video generation to protect character identity.
required_reference_views: prop close-up of the paper crane

character_model_sheet.purpose: Generate this character turnaround model sheet before any scene image.
character_model_sheet.character_reference_prompt:
# Character Turnaround Model Sheet Prompt

Create the master character turnaround model sheet for the entire music video. This model sheet will be attached as the identity reference for every later scene image, and optionally as a secondary reference for video generation.

Required views:
- front full-body view
- left side full-body view
- right side full-body view
- back full-body view
- three-quarter full-body view
- face close-up
- prop close-up of the paper crane

Character design: solitary anime teenager carrying a folded paper crane, late-teen anime character, stylized and non-photorealistic, short layered black hair with a long side fringe, slightly wind-tossed, dark school coat over a white shirt, narrow tie, simple shoulder bag, slim figure, coat hem and side fringe readable in silhouette. Signature prop: small paper crane. Accent detail: neon magenta/cyber pink is dominant, with deep plum shadows, subtle icy cyan reflections, and silver-white rim highlights. Visual world: dark cyber noir anime with neon magenta and cyber pink dominance.

Composition: clean anime production model sheet, neutral simple background, aligned character views at the same scale, full body visible from head to toe in each turnaround pose, clear face close-up, clear hairstyle silhouette, clear outfit seams, clear shoulder bag shape, clear paper crane prop, no dramatic camera angle, no cropped limbs, no environmental scene, no action pose. limited-color cyber anime palette: neon magenta/cyber pink dominant, deep plum/dark violet shadows, graphite gray/near-black backgrounds, subtle icy cyan reflections only, and silver-white highlights. Non-photorealistic, no live action, no text, no watermark.

Consistency rules:
- Keep the same hairstyle in every scene.
- Keep the same outfit and shoulder bag in every scene.
- Preserve the paper crane as the recurring symbol.
- Use anime cinematic styling, never live-action realism.
- First create a full character turnaround model sheet from character_reference_prompt.md.
- The model sheet must show front, left side, right side, back, and three-quarter views.
- Attach that same character model sheet when generating every scene image.
- Attach the final scene image as the primary image-to-video reference when generating the video for that scene.
- If the video tool supports multiple references, also attach the character model sheet as a secondary identity reference.

Production workflow:
- Generate a clean character turnaround model sheet before scene production.
- The model sheet should include front, back, left side, right side, three-quarter view, full body, and face close-up references.
- Use the same model sheet as an input/reference for every scene image prompt.
- After each scene image is approved, use that scene image as the primary image-to-video input with the matching video prompt.
- When possible, include the model sheet as a secondary reference during video generation to protect character identity.
required_views: prop close-up of the paper crane
usage: Attach this model sheet as the identity reference for every scene image prompt.
Use each generated scene image as the primary image-to-video reference.
If the video tool supports multiple references, attach this model sheet as a secondary video reference.

scenes.protagonist_continuity: solitary anime teenager carrying a folded paper crane
scenes.story_beat_ko: Intro에서는 주인공이 비가 그친 조용한 도시 거리에 홀로 등장하며, 종이학과 빛의 규칙을 처음 보여줍니다.
scenes.story_beat_en: In the Intro, the protagonist appears alone in quiet city street after rain, introducing the paper crane and the visual rules of the world.
scenes.continuity_from_previous_ko: 첫 장면이므로 주인공의 고립감, 종이학, 네온 마젠타 중심의 사이버 도시 규칙을 분명히 소개합니다.
scenes.continuity_to_next_ko: 다음 Verse 장면으로 감정이 넘어가도록 시선, 발걸음, 빛의 방향을 이어 둡니다.
scenes.story_prompt_context: Narrative continuity: this is the opening beat of the music video. Continue from the previous scene through the same protagonist, paper crane, walking direction, rain reflections, and the fixed neon magenta cyber palette. Story beat: In the Intro, the protagonist appears alone in quiet city street after rain, introducing the paper crane and the visual rules of the world.
scenes.image_prompt: Use the attached character turnaround model sheet as the identity lock. Match the same face, hairstyle, outfit, body proportions, shoulder bag, silhouette, and signature paper crane exactly. Use the model sheet views front, side, back, and three-quarter as canonical design references. Only change pose, camera angle, expression, lighting, and environment for this scene. Do not invent new clothes, hair shape, accessories, or body proportions. Narrative continuity: this is the opening beat of the music video. Continue from the previous scene through the same protagonist, paper crane, walking direction, rain reflections, and the fixed neon magenta cyber palette. Story beat: In the Intro, the protagonist appears alone in quiet city street after rain, introducing the paper crane and the visual rules of the world. solitary anime teenager carrying a folded paper crane, short layered black hair with a long side fringe, slightly wind-tossed, dark school coat over a white shirt, narrow tie, simple shoulder bag, in quiet city street after rain, emotion: melancholic, dark cinematic cyber lighting with neon magenta glow, deep violet shadows, subtle icy cyan reflections, and silver rim light, wide cinematic silhouette shot with the protagonist small in frame, slow push-in through rainfall, dark cinematic anime music video frame, limited-color cyber anime palette: neon magenta/cyber pink dominant, deep plum/dark violet shadows, graphite gray/near-black backgrounds, subtle icy cyan reflections only, and silver-white highlights, scene color balance: mostly graphite gray and near-black, faint cyber pink glow, tiny icy cyan reflections, strong silhouette, soft film grain, manga panel atmosphere, glowing waveform-like light trails, high contrast atmospheric depth, non-photorealistic, no live action, no rainbow colors, no warm daylight palette, no natural pastel palette, no heavy lip sync, no text, no watermark
scenes.video_prompt: Use the attached scene image as the primary first-frame/image-to-video reference. If the tool supports multiple references, also attach the character turnaround model sheet as a secondary identity reference. Preserve the character identity from the model sheet and the scene image. Do not redesign the face, hair, outfit, shoulder bag, paper crane, body proportions, or color rules. Narrative continuity: this is the opening beat of the music video. Continue from the previous scene through the same protagonist, paper crane, walking direction, rain reflections, and the fixed neon magenta cyber palette. Story beat: In the Intro, the protagonist appears alone in quiet city street after rain, introducing the paper crane and the visual rules of the world. Anime cinematic music video shot. Maintain protagonist: short layered black hair with a long side fringe, slightly wind-tossed, dark school coat over a white shirt, narrow tie, simple shoulder bag. Scene in quiet city street after rain with melancholic mood. Camera: slow push-in through rainfall; composition: wide cinematic silhouette shot with the protagonist small in frame. Atmosphere: rain, dark cyber depth, neon magenta/cyber pink glow, deep plum shadows, subtle icy cyan reflections, silver-white rim highlights. Scene color balance: mostly graphite gray and near-black, faint cyber pink glow, tiny icy cyan reflections. Keep motion slow and emotional, avoid dialogue and lip sync, preserve the fixed channel color palette, use a clean cinematic transition at the end.

scenes.lyrics_excerpt:
괜히 더 크게 웃어본다
아무 일 없는 척 걸어본다
친구들 말에 고개 끄덕여도
내 맘은 자꾸 늦게 온다

scenes.lyrics_excerpt:
아픈 맘 들킬까 고개 든다
괜히 더 밝은 척 또 웃는다

혼자만 조용히
